hypothesis:
## Novel Hypothesis

Incorporating dynamic, timestep-dependent constraint weighting schedules (where α1 and α2 vary as functions of diffusion timestep t) will enable PCDiff to achieve superior exploration of low-density regions in the compositional design space compared to static regularization, thereby increasing the discovery rate of novel composite formulations with extreme property combinations (e.g., simultaneously maximized yield strength and thermal conductivity) that lie near the boundaries of physical feasibility.
